title: BlackBerry Earnings Preview: How Will Q2 Turn Out
text: BlackBerry Limited was slated to report second quarter 2020 earnings results on September 24, before the opening bell. Analysts expected the company to report a loss of $0.01 per share for the quarter, compared to earnings of $0.04 per share the previous year. Revenue was expected to grow 24% year-over-year to $265.7 million. BlackBerry expected total revenue to grow 23-27% year-over-year on a non-GAAP basis, driven by a double-digit percentage increase in billings. Within this, IoT revenue was expected to grow 12-16% year-over-year while BlackBerry Cylance revenue was expected to grow 25-30%. BlackBerry's shares had gained 6% year-to-date and 9% in the past one month, with the majority of analysts giving the stock a Hold rating and an average price target of $7.95.
